feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Physical media chads, how are we holding up?
entry.content: Physical media chads, how are we holding up?

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Social media and dating apps ruined everything, now only 6'3 male model gigachad…
entry.content: Social media and dating apps ruined everything, now only 6'3 male model gigachads with 8x6 penises will be able to get laid. Even regular Chads are incels now and you think you have a chance? Even handsome Chads get rejected for only being 5'11. And even tall handsome Chads get laughed out of the bedroom for not being over 7 inches in length and 5.5 in girth. Tinder, onlyfans, social media, women fucking themselves with giant dildos and having sex with hung gigachads... it's honestly just over.

entry.content: and so have I

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: The best part about making meatballs is knowing that /v/ doesn’t know how to. An…
entry.content: The best part about making meatballs is knowing that /v/ doesn’t know how to. And it is important to make meatballs so that you can remind /v/ that they aren’t allowed to have anything good in life. They’re allowed to have console wars and demoralization threads, and GTA6 spam. And, you know, that’s pretty much it

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Switch Lite.
entry.content: Curious to hear from anyone who picked one of these up, I'm aware of the drift issue but I can always send it to Nintendo for repairs if need be. I've recently had a lot of hospital appointments and I'd like to get one of these as a cheap inconsequential means of playing games while waiting so I'm curious how the experience is and any game recommendations that are well suited for it.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >Yahtzee is going to review Slay the Princess expect a flood of normalfags in th…
entry.content: >Yahtzee is going to review Slay the Princess expect a flood of normalfags in the coming weeks

entry.title: /news/ News: US job drops sharply in June; labor force participation rate 5-year low
entry.content: https://www.reuters.com/world/us/us -job-growth-misses-expectations-jun e-unemployment-rate-falls-42-2026-0 7-02/ U.S. job growth slowed sharply in June and payroll gains for the prior two months were revised lower, pointing to a cooling labor market and prompting financial markets to dial back expectations for a near-term interest rate hike from the Federal Reserve. While the Labor Department's closely watched employment report on Thursday showed the unemployment rate dropped to 4.2% last month

entry.title: /int/ International: In my mid-sized city, the most exciting that has happened in the past 3 years is…
entry.content: In my mid-sized city, the most exciting that has happened in the past 3 years is a Uniqlo location opening. Before that, it was Costco opening a gas station and a T&T Supermarket opening that were the most exciting things ever to happen. Is it like this in your country too?

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: I never watch anything made before 2010. What am I missing out on?
entry.content: I never watch anything made before 2010. What am I missing out on?

entry.title: /b/ Random: Bill and Hillary Clinton recline in rocking chairs separated (purposefully) by a…
entry.content: Bill and Hillary Clinton recline in rocking chairs separated (purposefully) by a little wicker coffee table on the front porch of a secret Virginia property, watching angry black clouds approaching from the east. "You know what I love?" Bill asks in his endearingly bumbling southern drawl. "Moments like this," he answers without a response. "Storm coming in, but we're alright. Get to watch it with my old lady who's seen me through thick and thin," he wheezes. Hillary smiles emptily. "You know wh

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: New trailer for action thriller MOTOR CITY starring Alan Ritchson
entry.content: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t6R klhKu9os >In 1970s Detroit, mechanic John Miller (Alan Ritchson) falls in love with Sophia (Shailene Woodley), the ex-girlfriend of ruthless mobster Reynolds (Ben Foster), who frames him for a crime he did not commit. After six years in prison, Miller breaks out with the help of his old army companions and embarks on a brutal campaign of vengeance to reclaim his life. The gimmick is that the movie is mostly dialogue-free, with only five lines of dialogue throu

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Emulation is better then physical now
entry.content: Just look at this, i dont even think i can putted my eyeballs on that many PS1 discs in real life but with emulatorion I can.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: BLAME THE SUITS
entry.content: The American model of game development is crumbling. >spend 8 years making absolute dogshit that gets a 63 on metacritic >work maybe an hour a day >when the publisher steps in to question why the fuck you haven’t done anything, cry about crunch and an abusive work environment Okay fine, clearly this isn’t working. You can’t do your job so we’re cutting you loose. >NOOOOO YOU CANT DO THAT ITS YOUR FAULT THAT WE FAILED! OUR 200M BUDGET AND 8 YEAR DEVELOPMENT TIMELINE FOR A 63 METACRITIC GAME IS YO

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: [Despairing News] Sony is hiding after backlash
entry.content: >Sony has seemingly abandoned all of its social media accounts after today’s controversial decision to stop manufacturing physical games in 2028. >Going off the recent reports, it seems like Sony wasn't expecting this level of backlash >For example, the official Sony account posted a simple tweet teasing the upcoming release of the next Spider-Man movie. Normally, this account gets a few hundred replies at most, but the tweet now has over 3,000 replies, and most of them are from people yelling a

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Why are movies so full of propaganda against the death penalty while at the same…
entry.content: Why are movies so full of propaganda against the death penalty while at the same time constantly glorifying violence. For sex crimes, repeat offenders and murder the only way to handle them is to remove them. There is no restitution or rehabilitation they are broken on the genetic and social level and allowing them to exist in the same system as those we think could be fixed is a moral and cognition hazard to other prisoners and the guards. Accidently killing an innocent man will do less damage

entry.title: /mu/ Music: The greatness of New Wave
entry.content: Fuck Michael Jackson. Fuck Elvis. Fuck the Beatles. None made the perfect song with the best production value, riff, and themes that encapsulated a century. All of that derived from black music; New Wave was the true Faustian Occidental genre when classical was dusty; even if the bands were liberal it didn’t matter because you could hear it in their soul. https://youtu.be/A0cMu3fhw2M?is=jEz tMW08h4ZMzwfi

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Hi /o. Need some advice on what to do. I own a 2005 Ford Escape that started to …
entry.content: Hi /o. Need some advice on what to do. I own a 2005 Ford Escape that started to give me trouble. Loud banging under the pedals that would make the vehicle shake. Took it in to find out what the issue is, and was told it was the transmission. That it would cost more than the vehicle is worth to fix. But I can still drive it. Last night the banging got louder, and my left front tire flew off and went across the road. Axel got scraped up bad. Bolts all missing. What I want to know is, did the faili

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Portrait, Antichrist, and Mechanical Animals were his classic albums. Each one w…
entry.content: Portrait, Antichrist, and Mechanical Animals were his classic albums. Each one was different, which was "best" largely depends on what you are into. Holy Wood tried to blend elements of Antichrist and MA but came off as bland and forced. He tried to do something interesting with GAoG but it didn't have the same spark as his earlier work. After that he settled into being a nostalgia act. The new music since then has been acceptable enough but mostly forgettable, just another industrial/alt/metal

A thread for prompters, editors, requesters, gooners, AI pornmommies, PLAPpers, Stable Diffusion directors, NovelAI artists—anything and everything AIslop. No limits past site rules; use Catbox galleries and uploads if needed. Keep dumps to a minimum and don't be a derailing cunt, thanks. entry.title: /o/ Auto: This Week on Sarah-N-Tuned
entry.content: Sarah reviews the "hardcore" Toyota Tacoma TRD Pro. She's finally not wearing denim and appears to be wearing a more playful outfit than usual consisting of a white patterned mini-skirt and bra combo with a half-shirt that is semi-sheer worn over. This video has over 100k views now. https://youtu.be/DdBTSA-3Tro It looks like she got her hair done but the color is a mess and her ends are split. I wish I could help her. Ok back to the fish taco (as she refers to it): it has a PLASTIC skidplate ove

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >save your MMO which has being dead for more than 15 years Seriously, why are th…
entry.content: >save your MMO which has being dead for more than 15 years Seriously, why are they doing this? How are they still around? They are relic from an almost defunct time of games from the 2000s and early 2010s. Are servers really cheap or is there some suspiciously wealthy whales still funding dead MMOs?

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Fuck man
entry.content: I want to go back in time. Everything is soulless now. We can't even have physical fucking games anymore!!!! And the saddest part is there are faggets out there that grew up in this shitty soulless environment that will defend the corporations that are willing to risk the entire hobby for a little bit of extra money. Idgaf about 99% of games but the fact that I can no longer buy collectible editions of games i do care about is infuriating me.

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: people accept the army too much. It's weird how people still fall in line and co…
entry.content: people accept the army too much. It's weird how people still fall in line and comply, rank and file to the orders and commands of the military. I just find this interesting because until the military itself changes, any attempt to liberate or free a society of people will falter and fail unless the military changes along with it. The military it still not really liberal in most parts of the world. It's just weird how people feign social progress, only to fall in line rank and file to the demands

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: What an absolute shitshow. It's official: James Cameron is completely out of thi…
entry.content: What an absolute shitshow. It's official: James Cameron is completely out of things to say. This movie is basically the exact same as Avatar 2, except with a new clan and more Spider bullshit. How could a master storyteller waste so much time to make the same movie twice? To be clear, I kinda like 1 and 2, despite their flaws.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: OPSEC DEMONS of the US Military
entry.content: You can buy precise location data of US Military Personnel on the field if you have a credit card, and despite several warnings by multiple security experts, contractors and even by the research division of the fucking Army they haven't chosen to make reforms. >In 2023, the Army paid to have the threat spelled out. Researchers at Duke University—working under a grant from the US Military Academy at West Point—set out to buy data on American service members the way a foreign adversary might. They

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Why didn't anyone shill this to me earlier? This show is amazing. The plot and c…
entry.content: Why didn't anyone shill this to me earlier? This show is amazing. The plot and characters are great, the premise is one of the more interesting takes on AI. It doesn't take itself way too seriously either. Anyone else watch it recently? Maybe I just had low expectations going in, but I love the show.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: ITT: Musicians with strange post-music careers
entry.content: The frontwoman of the twee band Heavely became an economist and got a CBE >Fletcher read economics at St Edmund Hall, Oxford.[4] In 1993, she completed a PhD in economics at Nuffield College, Oxford titled 'Theories of Self-Regulation'. In 2001, she was appointed Chief Economist at the Office of Fair Trading[5] and in 2008 took on the additional role of Senior Director of Mergers.[6] She left in April 2013, to become Professor of Competition Policy at the University of East Anglia.[7] She has be

Now, here is the full version of the video: https://litter.catbox.moe/l84un481v 6w0dbsm.mp4 This one is quite long (6 minutes) and it's the video that took me the longest to make. entry.title: /int/ International: Do your culture have this practice? Whenever I visited China, when it came time …
entry.content: Do your culture have this practice? Whenever I visited China, when it came time to pay for the bill at a restaurant, my parents and my uncles/aunts would start fighting for the bill. Like sometimes they would even manhandle/wrestle each other to get to the bill first so they could pay. Also, whenever somebody offered a gift to someone, you'd have to do this whole tango of >take it >no i can't >ahh please just take it >no it's really too much, you shouldn't have >ahh just take it.... repeat 5x un

entry.title: /o/ Auto: New Jeep Cherokee - 0-60 in 6.3 but 5-60 (rolling start) 7.6 engine is 2 boosty …
entry.content: New Jeep Cherokee - 0-60 in 6.3 but 5-60 (rolling start) 7.6 engine is 2 boosty and 2 smol gets pretty good fuel economy but interior is cheap plastic fantastic with only touch controls to adjust the temp, the air suspension gives a firm ride not good! I remember reading a vintage review of a car that had a 2 second gap between 0-60 and 5-60 I wish I could remember what it was just a few days ago. https://www.caranddriver.com/review s/a71536995/2026-jeep-grand-cheroke e-summit-test/ that said, I

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Hi everyone, I'm buying my first car—a 1988 Mazda 626—for $230.
entry.content: Hi everyone. I'm buying my first car—a 1988 Mazda 626—for $230. I live in Russia and decided to go with a Japanese car because I'm a huge fan of their vehicles and culture. This is my first time on 4chan as a participant rather than just an observer, so I'm excited to make my first post. What advice can you give me on getting it roadworthy? The car starts but won't move; I'm having it towed over on the 20th.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: EPP — addressing "retarded LaTeX" and the compression argument
entry.content: been making EPP threads here. previous ones archived. two arguments keep coming up worth addressing properly. %epp=0.3% @heading "EPP vs LaTeX" {bold} @text "plain text. no compiler. reads in notepad." 1. "EPP is a retarded LaTeX with retarded syntax" LaTeX embeds images, needs a compiler, has a Turing-complete macro system and a 4GB install. EPP is plain UTF-8 you can read in notepad and parse in 150 lines. calling EPP a retarded LaTeX is like calling a bicycle a retarded car. also LaTeX suppor

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Why is death metal said to be the spiritual successor to classical music? It mig…
entry.content: Why is death metal said to be the spiritual successor to classical music? It might be fast, but when you learn the guitar you realise that it is one of the most brainless genres out there. 99% of it is just chugging the bottom two strings with no chord progressions to speak of (for rhythm) and playing scales up and down real fast (for lead). Whatever the fuck the vocalist is doing can't even be called music.

entry.content: Was using Bullguard for years, was able to get a year for $5, so preloaded a bunch of years, then they became Norton, and now my subscription has run out. I'm just using Windows Defender right now, but don't know how good it actually is, also I'm on Windows 10 so now that support has ended I don't know how that will affect it tl;dr wut anti-veerus is gud?

entry.title: /news/ News: Turtle tits-up
entry.content: https://www.yahoo.com/news/us/artic les/ems-called-mitch-mcconnells-hom e-235831905.html Washington — On the same morning Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell was hospitalized last month, emergency medical personnel went to his home to respond to an unconscious person who appeared to experience "cardiac arrest," according to a public EMS dispatch call reviewed by CBS News. During the recording, a dispatcher called in a "cardiac arrest" and a medic said there was "CPR in progress" at McConnell's addre
